title: descurainia rubescens
intro: Descurainia rubescens, also known as western honewort, is a perennial plant that belongs to the mustard family (Brassicaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Western honewort can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used topically to treat skin conditions, wounds, and burns.
